tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine in blood and urine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, and abnormal urinalysis (cloudy, brown urine, low specific gravity, glucosuria, ketonuria, bilirubinuria). Electrolytes and lipids are within normal limits. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with glycosuria unrelated to hyperglycemia, raising suspicion for renal glycosuria or tubular dysfunction. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
